# NEXO Brain

> Open-source cognitive memory system for AI agents (v1.7.0)

NEXO Brain gives AI agents persistent memory using the Atkinson-Shiffrin cognitive architecture. It provides 115+ MCP tools across 22 categories including semantic RAG, knowledge graph-boosted search, HNSW vector indexing, trust scoring, episodic memory, claim graph, metacognitive error prevention, inter-terminal coordination, web dashboard v2, natural memory decay, a full nervous system of 11 autonomous scripts, and 13 LaunchAgent templates.

## Key Features

- Three-store memory: Sensory Register, Short-Term Memory, Long-Term Memory
- Semantic vector search with fastembed (BAAI/bge-base-en-v1.5, 768 dimensions)
- Knowledge Graph-boosted search: structurally important memories rank higher
- HNSW vector indexing: optional hnswlib acceleration, auto-activates at 10,000+ memories
- Claim Graph: atomic verifiable claims with provenance, contradiction detection, verification status
- Ebbinghaus forgetting curve for natural memory pruning
- Discriminative sister memory fusion
- Cognitive dissonance detection
- Trust score (0-100) for internal rigor calibration
- Metacognitive Guard: pre-edit checks with learnings and blocking rules
- Episodic memory: change logs, decisions, session diaries with mental state continuity
- Inter-terminal coordination: auto-inbox via PostToolUse hook, claude_session_id tracking
- Web Dashboard v2: FastAPI with 6 pages (Overview, Operations, Calendar, Inbox), CRUD UI, sidebar nav, trust widget
- Nervous System: 11 autonomous scripts (immune, synthesis, self-audit, postmortem, dashboard, followup hygiene, GitHub monitor, catch-up, watchdog, evolution, cognitive decay, deep sleep)
- 13 LaunchAgent templates (macOS) + systemd user timers (Linux) + crontab fallback
- Cross-Platform: macOS (LaunchAgents), Linux (systemd/crontab), Windows (WSL) with full automated processes
- Modular architecture: db/ package (11 modules), cognitive/ package (6 modules)
- Adaptive Learned Weights: signal weights learn from real feedback via Ridge regression
- Somatic Markers: pain memory per file/area with risk scoring and validated recovery
- Adaptive Personality: 6 weighted signals (vibe, corrections, brevity, topic, tool errors, git diff)
- Evolution System: weekly self-improvement cycle with snapshot/rollback and circuit breakers
- Deep Sleep: overnight transcript analysis finds uncaptured corrections, protocol violations, missed commitments
- Watchdog Level 3: auto-reexecutes missed crons, verifies repairs with post-fix checks
- Migration script: automated v1.5 to v1.6 migration (13 DB migrations, i18n, logo optimization)
- 24 pytest tests with isolated databases
- Plugin system with hot-reload
- 100% local: SQLite + ONNX Runtime, zero cloud dependencies

## Installation

```
npx nexo-brain@1.7.0
```

## Documentation

- Website: https://nexo-brain.com
- Changelog: https://nexo-brain.com/changelog/
- Docs: https://nexo-brain.com/docs/
- GitHub: https://github.com/wazionapps/nexo
- Wiki: https://github.com/wazionapps/nexo/wiki
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/nexo-brain

## Architecture

Based on the Atkinson-Shiffrin model from cognitive psychology:
1. Sensory Register captures raw input (30s TTL, attention filter)
2. Short-Term Memory holds working context (rehearsal resets decay)
3. Long-Term Memory consolidates via semantic similarity (permanent)

Modular code: db/ package (11 modules: core, fts, schema, sessions, reminders, learnings, credentials, tasks, entities, episodic, evolution) and cognitive/ package (6 modules: core, search, ingest, decay, trust, memory).

## API

NEXO Brain exposes all functionality as MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools. Compatible with Claude Code, OpenClaw, and any MCP client.

Tool categories: Cognitive (8), Cognitive Input (5), Cognitive Advanced (8), Guard (5), Episodic (12), Sessions (5), Coordination (7), Reminders (5), Followups (4), Learnings (5), Credentials (5), Task History (3), Menu (1), Entities (5), Preferences (4), Agents (5), Backup (3), Evolution (5), Adaptive & Somatic (4), Knowledge Graph (4), Claim Graph (6), Artifacts (5), Context Continuity (2).
